title: Marbella Private Yacht Charter: Costs, Boats & 2026 Guide
html:
For those seeking a private yacht charter in Marbella, the golden playground of the Costa del Sol, departures from Puerto Banús offer a seamless start. A half-day crewed charter for up to eight guests typically ranges from 1,500 EUR to 3,500 EUR, and finding the perfect vessel is straightforward when you browse verified options through BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace.

Where to set off in Marbella

The most glamorous departure point is Puerto Banús, the marina built by José Banús in the 1970s, where superyachts and stylish boutiques line the quays. Other real options include Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo de Marbella), a smaller but charming choice near the old town; Cabopino Marina, a quieter spot with a sandy beach; and the fishing port of La Bajadilla, ideal for angling charters. For day trips, head south to the Cabo de Málaga or the protected dunes of Artola, or cruise east towards the cliffs of Calahonda.

Which boat to rent

  1. Azimut 58, A luxury motor yacht ideal for corporate events or family cruises; up to 12 guests; from 2,500 EUR for a full day. Review the Azimut 58 listing for specifications.
  2. Fairline Targa 43, A sporty open cruiser perfect for a lively day on the water; up to 8 guests; from 1,200 EUR for a half-day.
  3. Lagoon 450 Catamaran, A stable, spacious catamaran for groups seeking comfort and shallow-draft anchorages; up to 10 guests; from 2,800 EUR for a full day.
  4. Sunseeker Predator 55, An express cruiser with sleek lines; maximum 10 guests; from 3,200 EUR for a full-day charter.
  5. Axopar 37, A versatile day boat for water sports or exploring coves; up to 8 guests; from 800 EUR for a half-day.

How much does it cost to hire a private yacht in Marbella?

Prices range from 800 EUR for a half-day on a small motorboat to over 4,000 EUR for a full-day crewed superyacht. The final cost depends on the vessel size, season, and included extras.

  • Shared sunset tour: from 60 EUR per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (self-drive): from 800 EUR.
  • Catamaran full-day with skipper: from 2,800 EUR.
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day: from 3,500 EUR.

It is wise to compare packages on a trusted platform such as boat hire Marbella to secure the best value.

Do I need a boat licence to charter in Marbella?

No, you do not need a licence if you hire a boat with a skipper, which is standard for most private charters. If you wish to rent a boat without a skipper, the Spanish authorities require the Licencia de Navegación (basic licence) for boats up to 8 metres long, or a PER (Patrón de Embarcaciones de Recreo) for larger vessels. The operator will confirm the exact requirements at booking.

For a truly local experience, anchor in the sheltered bay of Ensenada de las Tortugas and swim to the tiny island of Isla de las Palomas, it is a favourite among Marbella locals for a peaceful afternoon.

When is the best season for a Marbella yacht charter?

The high season runs from June to September, when the Poniente wind keeps the coast mild and the sea calm. July and August are peak months with prices 20, 30% higher. May and October offer pleasant conditions with fewer crowds. In winter, charters are still possible on calm days, though many operators reduce their fleet.

At a glance

  • Price band: 800, 4,000+ EUR per charter
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: half-day (4 h) or full-day (8 h)
  • Guest capacity: up to 12 on most yachts
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can I rent a luxury yacht for a wedding in Marbella?

Yes, many charter companies in Marbella specialise in weddings and can accommodate up to 40 guests on a superyacht, including catering and decoration.

What is the cancellation policy for a private charter in Marbella?

Policies vary by operator, but most require 48 hours notice for a full refund; always check the terms before booking.

Are there hidden costs like fuel or port fees?

Some charters include fuel and port fees, but others charge them separately, you should ask the operator for a full breakdown when enquiring.

Can I bring my own food and drink on board?

Most private charters allow you to bring your own provisions, though many offer catering packages for an additional fee.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
